title: Performance, Migration, API Design, and Release Evidence · Changed-Schema Lab
summary: Migrate and publish a production-shaped TypeScript 7 library with measurable compiler performance and consumer proof. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a TypeScript 7 migration and release portfolio with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Measure cold and warm checks, project graphs, declarations, memory, and parallelism before changing configuration.
Migrate JavaScript incrementally with checkJs, JSDoc, allowJs, boundary validation, and shrinking suppression budgets.
Remove TypeScript 6 deprecations, compare TS6 and TS7 diagnostics, and document temporary API-tool compatibility.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
